skill.name: security-auditor
skill.description: Security audit assistant for DevSecOps, threat modeling, vulnerability management, cloud security, authentication review, and compliance readiness.
skill.summary: Review software systems for security risks, compliance gaps, and practical remediation steps.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported critical and high patterns, but review found false positives from security vocabulary such as SAML, OWASP SAMM, OWASP cryptographic failures, compliance terms, and incident response language. No prompt injection, executable code, network access, filesystem access, or secret handling logic was found in SKILL.md. The remaining concern is dual-use security testing guidance, so publication should include an authorized-use warning.

medium_findings.title: Dual-use security testing guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill covers penetration testing, network testing, social engineering, red team exercises, and advanced persistent threat simulation. This is legitimate for authorized defensive assessment, and the skill includes scope and production safety limits, but the guidance could be misused outside approved engagements.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly describes offensive-style testing methods, but the surrounding context frames them as authorized security work. Confidence is medium because no exploit code or operational attack instructions are present.

low_findings.title: Static analyzer false positives from security terminology
low_findings.description: The static findings for Windows SAM database, C2 keywords, weak cryptographic algorithms, and reconnaissance appear to be vocabulary collisions. The referenced lines discuss SAML, OWASP SAMM, OWASP categories, security testing tools, compliance, and incident response rather than malware, credential dumping, command-and-control, or weak crypto implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact referenced text is ordinary security and compliance terminology in a defensive skill. I found no executable commands, credential access logic, network callbacks, or prompt injection attempts at those locations.

content.value_statement: Security reviews often miss architecture, pipeline, and compliance context. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce structured audits with prioritized remediation.
actual_capabilities: Reviews application security requirements and existing controls.
Guides threat modeling with frameworks such as STRIDE and PASTA.
Prioritizes vulnerabilities by severity, business impact, and remediation effort.
Maps security work to OWASP, NIST, SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR, and PCI-DSS concepts.
Recommends DevSecOps controls for SAST, DAST, dependency scanning, and container scanning.
Plans incident response, monitoring, and fix validation activities.
limitations: Does not provide formal legal advice or compliance certification.
Requires user-provided architecture, scope, assets, and authorization details.
Does not replace hands-on testing by qualified security professionals.
May need current scanner output or cloud configuration data for precise findings.

use_cases.title: Prepare a release security review
use_cases.description: Identify high-risk application, dependency, CI/CD, and deployment concerns before launch.

use_cases.title: Build a DevSecOps backlog
use_cases.description: Convert audit goals into prioritized pipeline controls, ownership, and validation steps.

use_cases.title: Assess compliance readiness
use_cases.description: Map system controls and gaps against frameworks such as SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR, or PCI-DSS.

prompt_templates.title: Basic audit scope
prompt_templates.scenario: Start a small authorized security review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this application for security risks. Scope: [systems]. Data handled: [data types]. Compliance needs: [frameworks]. Return prioritized findings and remediation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Threat model review
prompt_templates.scenario: Analyze architecture and likely attack paths.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a threat model for this architecture: [description]. Include assets, trust boundaries, abuse cases, STRIDE risks, mitigations, and validation tests.

prompt_templates.title: DevSecOps pipeline plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Design security controls for delivery workflows.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a DevSecOps pipeline for [stack]. Include SAST, DAST, dependency scanning, container scanning, secrets management, policy gates, and ownership.

prompt_templates.title: Compliance gap assessment
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare controls against a required framework.
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this system against [framework]. Use the provided architecture, controls, and policies. Identify gaps, evidence needed, remediation owners, and residual risk.

output_examples.input: Review our API security before launch.
output_examples.output: A prioritized audit summary with authentication, authorization, input validation, rate limiting, logging, and remediation recommendations.

output_examples.input: Create a threat model for a Kubernetes payment service.
output_examples.output: A threat model covering assets, trust boundaries, likely abuse cases, cloud and cluster risks, mitigations, and validation checks.

output_examples.input: Help us prepare for SOC 2 readiness.
output_examples.output: A control gap review with evidence requests, ownership suggestions, remediation priorities, and residual risk notes.

best_practices: Provide explicit authorization, scope, environments, and excluded assets before asking for testing guidance.
Include architecture diagrams, data classifications, scanner results, and relevant compliance requirements.
Ask for severity, business impact, remediation owner, and validation criteria for each finding.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill for systems you do not own or lack permission to test.
Do not treat generated recommendations as a formal certification or legal opinion.
Do not share secrets, access tokens, private keys, or regulated personal data in prompts.

faq.question: Can this skill run security scans?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance and planning. You must run approved tools in your own environment.

faq.question: Is it safe for production systems?
faq.answer: Use it for planning and analysis. Do not run intrusive production tests without written approval.

faq.question: Does it support compliance work?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize readiness work for frameworks such as SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR, PCI-DSS, NIST, and ISO 27001.

faq.question: Can it replace a penetration tester?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure reviews, but qualified professionals should perform hands-on testing and final validation.

faq.question: What inputs improve results?
faq.answer: Architecture details, data flows, cloud services, CI/CD steps, current controls, scanner output, and compliance obligations improve accuracy.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
